title: Combat Management System Software Integration
entry_mechanism: Saudi software engineering team under Navantia technical supervision, working toward software modification capability
executive_summary:
The combat management system (CMS) is the digital brain of a warship — integrating sensors, weapons, communications, and navigation into a coherent tactical picture that enables command and weapons engagement decisions. For Saudi Arabia's Avante 2200 corvettes, the CMS is supplied by Navantia and integrates European sensor and weapon systems. Saudi Arabia currently has zero software engineering capability for naval CMS — every software update, configuration change, and integration task is performed by Navantia or its subcontractors.

Node NV-4 targets the establishment of a Saudi naval software engineering team capable of performing CMS integration tasks — adding new sensor interfaces, modifying tactical display configurations, and eventually developing Saudi-specific software modules — under Navantia technical supervision initially and with growing autonomy through Phase II and III. The parallel with Node MA-4 (UAV software) is exact: software ownership is the foundation of operational sovereignty.

The strategic importance is clear: a Saudi CMS software team can respond to Saudi-specific tactical requirements (Red Sea threat environment, Saudi command concepts) faster than a foreign OEM, and can modify the system without creating a foreign intelligence visibility risk.

companies.name: Navantia / Navantia Systems
companies.rationale: Navantia holds the Avante 2200 CMS and must provide SDK access as part of the programme. Navantia Systems is the software division that would manage the Saudi software team engagement.
companies.contact: Navantia Systems VP Saudi Arabia Programme

companies.name: Indra
companies.rationale: Indra develops naval combat management systems and has Saudi Arabia defence relationships. Non-ITAR Spanish technology with active Gulf engagement.
companies.contact: Indra VP Naval Systems, Gulf Region

companies.name: Systematic
companies.rationale: Systematic's SitaWare naval C2 framework could serve as the integration layer for Saudi-developed CMS modules, reducing dependency on Navantia's proprietary architecture.

companies.name: L3Harris
companies.rationale: L3Harris has naval electronics integration capability and some non-ITAR products suitable for Saudi CMS integration work. Limited by ITAR on most sensitive systems.

companies.name: Rohde & Schwarz
companies.rationale: R&S supplies naval communications systems and has SDK/API frameworks that Saudi engineers could integrate into the CMS. Non-ITAR German technology.
companies.contact: Rohde & Schwarz VP Naval and Government

partners.name: STC Solutions / Defence Division
partners.current: Large-scale software integration; government systems; some communications integration
partners.upgrade: Establish naval software division; NSCS security clearance; Navantia CMS SDK access; Saudi naval software engineer team (15 engineers, 2-year Navantia immersion)
partners.timeline: 36 months to first CMS modification capability

partners.name: Elm Company
partners.current: Complex government software platforms; Arabic-language systems; secure data platforms
partners.upgrade: Naval CMS data management module development; Arabic CMS interface development; NSCS clearance for classified naval systems

partners.name: KAUST Computer Science
partners.current: AI, machine learning, computer vision research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KAUST naval AI programme; CMS decision support algorithm research; Saudi-specific threat assessment modelling
partners.timeline: 24 months to research output usable in CMS

partners.name: KACST / Defence Research
partners.current: Signal processing, communications research, some naval electronics
partners.upgrade: Naval electronic warfare signal processing research relevant to CMS sensor integration; Saudi-specific electronic intelligence processing
